title: Traditional Paska Bread
description: A traditional Ukrainian sweet bread, perfect for special occasions. It's a rich, dense bread with a sweet and malty flavor.
instructions: 1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the warm water, yeast, and 1 teaspoon of sugar. Let it sit for 5-10 minutes until the yeast is activated.
2. Add the flour, remaining sugar, eggs, and salt to the bowl. Mix until a sticky dough forms.
3. Knead the dough for 10-15 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
4. Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for about 1 hour, or until it has doubled in size.
5. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
6. Punch down the dough and shape it into a round loaf. Place the loaf on the prepared baking sheet.
7. Brush the top of the loaf with the melted butter.
8. Bake the bread for 35-40 minutes, or until it's golden brown and sounds hollow when tapped.
9. Remove the bread from the oven and let it cool on a wire rack for at least 10-15 minutes before slicing.
10. Serve the paska warm or at room temperature.
